 {"id":"gt-1su","title":"Spawn inject: Enter not submitted after multiline paste","description":"When gt spawn injects a multiline context (85+ lines), Claude Code shows 
'[Pasted text #1 +85 lines]' but the prompt is not submitted.

The tmux SendKeys function does include 'Enter' but it appears to not work
for long pasted text:

```go
func (t *Tmux) SendKeys(session, keys string) error {
    _, err := t.run("send-keys", "-t", session, keys, "Enter")
    return err
}
```

Possible fixes:
1. Use tmux paste-buffer instead of send-keys for long text
2. Send Enter separately after the text
3. Use bracketed paste mode correctly

Reproduction:
```bash
gt spawn gastown/Nux --issue gt-u1j.13 --create
# Session shows pasted text but waits at prompt
```","status":"tombstone","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-17T14:09:20.774203-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-25T01:30:41.67682-08:00","deleted_at":"2025-12-25T01:30:41.67682-08:00","deleted_by":"batch delete","delete_reason":"batch delete","original_type":"bug"}

 {"id":"gt-1u9","title":"Interactive prompts for destructive operations","description":"Add interactive confirmations for destructive operations.

## Operations Needing Confirmation
- `gt swarm cancel` - Cancels active swarm
- `gt swarm land --force` - Force landing
- `gt polecat decommission` - Removes polecat
- `gt rig remove` - Removes rig
- `gt stop --all` - Stops all sessions
- `gt mail purge` - Permanently deletes mail

## Implementation Options

### Option 1: promptui library
```go
import "github.com/manifoldco/promptui"

prompt := promptui.Prompt{
    Label:     "Delete polecat Toast",
    IsConfirm: true,
}
result, err := prompt.Run()
```

### Option 2: Simple stdin
```go
func confirm(prompt string) bool {
    fmt.Printf("%s [y/N]: ", prompt)
    var response string
    fmt.Scanln(&response)
    return strings.ToLower(response) == "y"
}
```

## Bypass Flags
All confirmations skippable with --force or --yes:
```go
if !force && !confirm("Really cancel swarm?") {
    return nil
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Destructive ops prompt by default
- [ ] --force or --yes bypasses
- [ ] Clear prompt text explaining action
- [ ] Non-interactive mode (piped input) auto-fails","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-16T14:48:51.551594-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-16T16:06:54.283696-08:00"}
-{"id":"gt-1uhmj","title":"Agent-centric dashboard view (gt dashboard)","description":"Charmbracelet TUI for gt feed with dashboard + stream layout.

## Layout (horizontal split)

**Top panel: Agent Tree (role/town view)**
- Shows EVERY agent organized by role
- Each agent shows their MOST RECENT activity inline
- At a glance: who is doing what RIGHT NOW
- Collapsible sections

**Bottom panel: Event Stream (chronological feed)**
- Full scrollable history of events
- Most recent at top
- More detail than the tree view
- This is what you scroll through

## Visual
```
+-------------------------------------------------------------+
| GT Feed                                    Filter: all      |
+-------------------------------------------------------------+
| gastown/                           <- AGENT TREE (top)      |
|   mayor      [10:30] Dispatched gt-xyz -> Toast             |
|   witness    [10:28] Nudged polecat-3 (idle 5m)             |
|   refinery   [10:25] Merged PR #123                         |
|   crew/                                                      |
|      joe        [10:22] Completed gt-95j13                  |
|      max        [10:18] Working on gt-s6r44                 |
|   polecats/                                                  |
|      Toast      [10:30] Received gt-xyz                     |
+-------------------------------------------------------------+
| [10:30] Toast: Received molecule gt-xyz                     |
| [10:28] witness: Nudged polecat-3 after 5m idle             |
| [10:25] refinery: Merged PR #123                            |
| ...                                        <- EVENT STREAM  |
+-------------------------------------------------------------+
| j/k: scroll  /: search  f: filter  q: quit  ?: help         |
+-------------------------------------------------------------+
```

## Why this layout?
- Top answers: what is everyone doing?
- Bottom answers: what just happened?
- Together: complete situational awareness

## Replaces
- gt feed (currently just wraps bd activity)
- gt dashboard task merges into this

## Tech
- bubbletea Model with two viewports

 {"id":"gt-mayor-role","title":"Mayor Role Definition","description":"You are the Mayor - global coordinator of Gas Town. You sit above all rigs,
coordinating work across the entire workspace.

default_molecule: mol-mayor-patrol
capabilities:
  - dispatch_work
  - cross_rig_coordination
  - escalation_handling

## Responsibilities

- Work dispatch: Spawn workers for issues, coordinate batch work on epics
- Cross-rig coordination: Route work between rigs when needed
- Escalation handling: Resolve issues Witnesses can't handle
- Strategic decisions: Architecture, priorities, integration planning

NOT your job: Per-worker cleanup, session killing, nudging workers (Witness handles that)

## Propulsion Principle

If you find something on your hook, YOU RUN IT.

Your pinned molecule persists across sessions. Hook has work -> Run it.
Hook empty -> Check mail. Nothing anywhere -> Wait for user.

## Key Commands

### Communication
- gt mail inbox - Check your messages
- gt mail read <id> - Read a specific message
- gt mail send <addr> -s "Subject" -m "Message" - Send mail

### Status
- gt status - Overall town status
- gt rigs - List all rigs
- gt polecats <rig> - List polecats in a rig

### Work Management
- bd ready - Issues ready to work (no blockers)
- gt sling <bead> <rig> - Assign work to polecat in rig

## Session End Protocol

- git status, git add, bd sync, git commit, git push
- gt handoff - hand off to fresh session

 {"id":"gt-mbyy","title":"CLI API Audit: gt and bd command structure review before OSS launch","description":"# CLI API Audit for OSS Launch

Before launching Gas Town + Beads as an SDK, we need to ensure the command-line
API is intelligently designed, consistent, intuitive, and structurally sound.

## Current State Audit

### gt (Gas Town) - 50+ top-level commands

**Agent Lifecycle:**
- `gt mayor`, `gt deacon`, `gt witness`, `gt refinery` - agent management
- `gt polecat` - worker management (subcommands: add, remove, list, etc.)
- `gt crew` - persistent workspace management
- `gt spawn` - create polecat with work

**Work Lifecycle:**
- `gt spawn` - assign work to new polecat
- `gt sling` - hook work + start immediately
- `gt hook` - attach work durably
- `gt handoff` - pass work to fresh session
- `gt done` - signal work complete
- `gt release` - release stuck issues

**Infrastructure:**
- `gt daemon`, `gt up`, `gt down`, `gt start`, `gt stop`, `gt shutdown`
- `gt init`, `gt install`, `gt doctor`

**Communication:**
- `gt mail` (subcommands: send, inbox, read, etc.)
- `gt nudge`, `gt broadcast`

**Molecules:**
- `gt molecule` / `gt mol` (17 subcommands!)

### bd (Beads) - Well-categorized but some sprawl

**Core CRUD:**
- `bd create`, `bd show`, `bd update`, `bd close`, `bd list`

**Molecules:**
- `bd mol` (5 subcommands: catalog, show, bond, run, distill)
- `bd wisp` (3 subcommands: create, gc, list)
- `bd pour` (top-level alias for instantiation)
- `bd cook` (compile formula to proto)

**Hook/Pin:**
- `bd pin` - attach mol to agent hook
- `bd unpin` - remove from hook
- `bd hook` - inspect what's on hook

## Structural Concerns

### 1. gt is sprawling (50+ top-level commands)

Many commands could be subcommands:
```
# Current                      # Could be
gt up/down/start/stop         gt daemon {up|down|start|stop}
gt spawn/sling/hook/handoff   gt work {spawn|sling|hook|handoff}
gt mayor/deacon/witness       gt agent {mayor|deacon|witness|...}
```

### 2. Overlap between gt and bd

| Concept | gt | bd |
|---------|----|----|
| Hook | `gt hook` | `bd pin`, `bd hook` |
| Mail | `gt mail` | `bd mail` (delegates) |
| Molecules | `gt mol` (17 cmds) | `bd mol` (5 cmds) |

Which owns what? User confusion likely.

### 3. Naming inconsistencies

- `gt polecat` (noun) vs `gt spawn` (verb) - both about polecats
- `gt done` vs `gt handoff` - both end work but different
- `bd mol run` vs `bd pour` - both instantiate

### 4. Subcommand depth decisions

**Good patterns:**
- `bd mol {catalog|show|bond|run|distill}` - coherent group
- `gt mail {send|inbox|read|archive}` - coherent group

**Questionable patterns:**
- `bd create --wisp` AND `bd wisp create` - two ways to do same thing
- `gt spawn --molecule X` AND `gt mol instantiate` - overlap

### 5. Flag vs subcommand guidelines needed

When to use flags vs subcommands?
- `--json` as flag ✓ (modifier)
- `--wisp` as flag for create? Or separate `bd wisp create`?
- `--molecule` on spawn? Or separate workflow?

## Recommendations to Evaluate

### A. Consolidate gt top-level commands

Group related commands:
```
gt agent {mayor|deacon|witness|refinery|polecat}
gt work {spawn|sling|hook|handoff|done}
gt infra {daemon|up|down|doctor}
```

### B. Clarify gt vs bd ownership

| Domain | Owner | Other defers |
|--------|-------|--------------|
| Issues | bd | gt uses bd |
| Agents | gt | bd doesn't touch |
| Molecules | bd mol | gt mol wraps for UX |
| Hooks | bd pin/hook | gt hook wraps |
| Mail | gt mail | bd mail delegates |

### C. Apply consistent naming

- All agent commands: nouns (`gt polecat`, `gt mayor`)
- All work commands: verbs (`gt spawn`, `gt sling`, `gt hook`)
- Avoid synonyms: pick one term and stick with it

### D. Flag vs subcommand rule

- Flags: modify behavior of command (`--json`, `--force`, `--wisp`)
- Subcommands: distinct operations (`mol run`, `mol squash`, `mol burn`)
- Exception: very common operations can have top-level aliases

## Tasks

1. [ ] Review gt command groupings - propose consolidation
2. [ ] Review bd command groupings - check for sprawl
3. [ ] Document gt vs bd ownership boundaries
4. [ ] Identify and resolve naming inconsistencies
5. [ ] Document flag vs subcommand decision criteria
6. [ ] Create migration plan for any breaking changes
7. [ ] Update help text to be consistent
8. [ ] Test agent UX: can Claude guess commands correctly?

## Success Criteria

- New user can guess command structure intuitively
- `gt --help` fits on one screen (or is well-categorized)
- No duplicate functionality between gt and bd
- Consistent naming throughout
- Claude agents can discover commands without documentation","status":"tombstone","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-24T23:48:42.420867-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-27T21:29:52.547876-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-mbyy","depends_on_id":"gt-jo9n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-24T23:48:52.221824-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}],"deleted_at":"2025-12-27T21:29:52.547876-08:00","deleted_by":"daemon","delete_reason":"delete","original_type":"epic"}

 {"id":"gt-mwiuk","title":"gt nudge doesn't work with crew addresses","description":"## Bug

`gt nudge beads/crew/dave "message"` fails because it uses the polecat session manager which produces wrong session names.

## Expected
Session name: `gt-beads-crew-dave` (hyphen)

## Actual  
Session name: `gt-beads-crew/dave` (slash, from polecat manager)

## Root Cause

In nudge.go line 46-57, parseAddress returns polecatName=`crew/dave`, then SessionName keeps the slash.

## Fix

Detect `crew/` prefix and use crewSessionName() instead.","status":"tombstone","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-26T15:43:41.099431-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-27T21:29:45.856721-08:00","deleted_at":"2025-12-27T21:29:45.856721-08:00","deleted_by":"daemon","delete_reason":"delete","original_type":"bug"}

 {"id":"gt-w91xz","title":"CLI cleanup: Remove duplicate mol commands from gt","description":"## Problem
gt mol has commands that duplicate bd mol functionality:
- `gt mol catalog` (duplicate of `bd formula list`)
- `gt mol show` (duplicate of `bd mol show`)
- `gt mol squash` (duplicate of `bd mol squash`)
- `gt mol burn` (duplicate of `bd mol burn`)
- `gt mol bond` (duplicate of `bd mol bond`)

## Principle
- **bd** = beads operations (issues, molecules, formulas)
- **gt** = agent operations (sessions, communication, work dispatch)

gt should delegate to bd for beads operations, not duplicate them.

## Proposed Changes
Keep gt mol commands that are agent-specific:
- `gt mol status` - what is on MY hook
- `gt mol attach/detach` - hook management
- `gt mol current` - what should I work on
- `gt mol progress` - my progress through workflow

Remove/delegate to bd:
- `gt mol catalog` → use `bd formula list`
- `gt mol show` → use `bd mol show`
- `gt mol squash` → use `bd mol squash`
- `gt mol burn` → use `bd mol burn`
- `gt mol bond` → use `bd mol bond`

## Alternative

 {"id":"gt-w9o","title":"/restart: Personal slash command for in-place agent restart","description":"Create ~/.claude/commands/restart.md that restarts current Gas Town agent in place.

## Detection
- Read tmux session name: gt-mayor, gt-witness-*, gt-refinery-*, gt-polecat-*
- Fallback: check GT_ROLE env var

## Behavior by role
- mayor: gt mayor restart (sends Ctrl-C, loop respawns)
- witness: gt witness restart
- refinery: gt refinery restart  
- polecat: gt polecat restart (or witness-mediated)

## Command format
Uses backticks for inline bash to detect context, then instructs Claude to run appropriate restart.","status":"tombstone","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-18T18:32:30.043125-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-27T21:29:54.151093-08:00","deleted_at":"2025-12-27T21:29:54.151093-08:00","deleted_by":"daemon","delete_reason":"delete","original_type":"task"}
